title: NMSC
description: NMSC (No More Sniffers/Camels) is a very simple datapack that just about removes the existence of sniffers and camels.
body:
# What is NMSC?

**NMSC** (No More Sniffers/Camels) is a small QoL datapack that helps with a problem I have found myself to have when i started playing 1.20+. That problem is the presence of sniffers and camels!

**NSMC** engages to follow its one and only policy, which is to rid any world of these two despicable mobs, and that's about it!


## But... why?

That is one hell of a question! Here's my answer:
My story starts long ago, in the far away time that was October 2022. Doomsday was on the 15th, it was the awful, *horrible*, ***HORRENDOUS*** day where it was announced by Mojang that there would be the addition of sniffers in the near future. I wished everyday for more time before my unfathomable fate arrived, but it was during June 7th of 2023 that all hell broke loose, and that's when 1.20 was released. With it came the camels, monsters that took refuge in the barren lands that are the deserts, but they didn't arrive alone. With them arrived the sniffers, who come from... water? But I would not have *ANY* of it! I wouldn't have their weird jumps nor their flowers! I would vow to myself that I would conquer them, and save all of the future Minecraft worlds. And that is where we stand today, for I have accomplished my mission that is to remove these amalgamations from our worlds.

TL:DR - I just don't really like the sniffer and camel because it looks oddly detailed compared to Minecraft's textures and since I'm not an artist, I just removed them from the game. On top of that I feel like sniffers don't really do anything and camels are just weird horses at this point. That's it.


## Before/After:

Camels:
![Before/After of camels](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/BsQNngrd/images/7db166f460874f8e81e1f9e2abea567f71d45268.jpeg)

Sniffers:
![Before/After of sniffers](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/BsQNngrd/images/be94bd75f4e43543991ad3f394957b9dec9529b1.jpeg)

## Will this be ported to other versions?

The modpack has now been updated to new versions thanks to a friend of mine! You can now enjoy my magnum opus on any versions from 1.20 up to 1.21.4!

Come on everyone, say it with me! "Thank you Bowswa!"

On another note, I plan to update this pack to 1.19 next! Coming soon in 2030 (maybe, if you're lucky).
